behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration: collectors + aggregator + summary input contract. A unit test of one function can miss wiring bugs (wrong field mapping, one source dropped). Real OS/network is not required; in-process fakes for the three sources are sufficient. No behavior text requires real model-output judgment or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline up to (but not including) summary generation with three source adapters stubbed to return distinct sentinel values; assert the payload handed to the summary step contains all three source sections and expected mapped keys/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The bug appears at interaction boundaries: source adapter error propagation + report composer fallback behavior. Use an in-process failing stub (exception/timeout error object) for one source and normal responses for others. System was considered, but rejected because the behavior text only requires graceful degradation semantics, not real network/OS failure characteristics. No LLM-output-quality dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Execute report generation with one source adapter configured to fail and two to succeed; assert run completes, output includes a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section, and other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Most direct reading is consistency between structured input and produced summary content (value fidelity), which can be tested with deterministic fixtures across summarizer + formatter modules. Agentic was considered but rejected because behavior text does not explicitly state a real LLM/ML call or non-deterministic model judgment. If implementation uses a real model for this step, this should be upgraded to Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Feed fixed input data with known key values through the summary-generation component and assert the resulting narrative contains the correct values/facts and no contradictions/omissions for those keys.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Upgrade to Agentic if summary-generation is backed by a real LLM API in production.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly crosses real infrastructure boundaries end-to-end, including outbound email transport and inbox receipt. In-process mocks can hide SMTP/auth/TLS/provider quirks and delivery-path failures. Agentic/Workflow not required by this behavior text because correctness target is delivery to inbox, not model decision quality across multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script (or equivalent full entrypoint) against test instances of the three data sources and a real email delivery path to a test mailbox; assert message is delivered to configured recipient, subject/body are present, and report content is intact.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real infra boundaries: unreachable email service conditions plus filesystem persistence across runs and retry behavior on subsequent invocation. Pure mocks can miss real I/O/error-shape issues and persistence semantics. Workflow was considered but rejected because this is retry/state progression across runs, not multiple agentic model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate a run where email endpoint is actually unreachable (e.g., refused connection) and verify report is written to disk; then run the scheduler entrypoint again with email restored and assert pending report is retried and delivered, with local fallback state cleared/updated appropriately.
